title: Floating Point Exception (SIGFPE) in DuckDB with complex window function and subqueries
description:
### What happens?

DuckDB crashes with a Floating Point Exception (SIGFPE) when executing a complex query involving `ARG_MIN`, `CASE`, `WINDOW FUNCTION`, and `SUBQUERIES`. 

The crash is reproducible in a Docker environment with ASAN enabled, but might not reproduce in a standard Release build if assertions are disabled or if the environment differs.

### To Reproduce

```sql
SELECT ARG_MIN ( -99999998430674944.000000 + x * 1 , CASE ( SELECT ( printf ( '%s' , NULL ) , ( SELECT 'zebra elephant tiger horse' NOT LIKE 'zebra%elephant%tiger%horse' ORDER BY gen_random_uuid ( ) ) ) FROM ( SELECT 0 UNION SELECT 1 UNION SELECT 2 UNION SELECT 3 ) , repeat ( 1 , 10000 ) ORDER BY argmax ( DISTINCT 'Äañt₡Xá你好世界我' , 72.000000 ) OVER ( ORDER BY - 1 DESC ROWS BETWEEN 6 FOLLOWING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING ) , - ( SELECT corr ( DISTINCT 1 , 1 ) ) DESC LIMIT 2 OFFSET 2 ) WHEN 8 THEN 'MED PKG' WHEN 2 THEN '29' ELSE 'MED P' END ) , IFNULL ( 20.500000 , 2000 ) FROM ( SELECT 0 AS x UNION SELECT 45 UNION SELECT 90 UNION SELECT 135 UNION SELECT ( SELECT NULL ) UNION SELECT 225 UNION SELECT 270 UNION SELECT 315 UNION SELECT 360 ) AS x ;
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 22.04 (Docker)

### DuckDB Version:

v0.10.1 (Source build with ASAN)

### DuckDB Client:

ODBC Driver (Reproduced via custom ODBC harness)

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

mao hg

### Affiliation:

Independent Researcher

key_bug_features: Complex query with ARG_MIN and window function
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT, description VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, description) VALUES (1, -99999998430674944.000000, 'Initial value'), (2, 20.5, 'Second value'), (3, NULL, 'Null value');
SELECT ARG_MIN(value + 1, CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 'NULL CASE' ELSE 'VALID CASE' END) FROM test_table;
SELECT id, value, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value > 0) AS positive_count FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT value, IFNULL((SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL), 0) AS avg_value FROM test_table ORDER BY value DESC;
